title: Invert Binary Tree
description:
Given the `root` of a binary tree, invert the tree, and return its root.

**Example:**
```
     4
   /   \
  2     7
 / \   / \
1   3 6   9
```
becomes:
```
     4
   /   \
  7     2
 / \   / \
9   6 3   1
```
hints: This is a classic problem. Maciejors once failed an interview at Google because he couldn't invert a binary tree on a whiteboard.
You need to swap the left and right child of every single node in the tree.
You can do this recursively: invert the left subtree, invert the right subtree, then swap them.
solution:
def invertTree(root):
    if not root: return None
    # Swap the children
    root.left, root.right = root.right, root.left
    # Recurse
    invertTree(root.left)
    invertTree(root.right)
    return root
